what_it_does: Implements the root App component that routes between welcome, project overview, wizard, and studio screens; manages project selection, wizard seeding, studio workspace navigation, Vi assistant panels, voice input, memory/identity overlay, Ask bar, and a tweak-driven theme/motion/feel system.
observations: Uses window globals (React, MS_DATA, MS_MOTION, MS_TOKENS, FlipBridge, ScreenGhost, ViContext, ViMemory, StreamHost) as integration points.
Holds a large state tree: screen routing, active project, overview tabs, studio workspace, zone memory, Vi/voice/memory toggles, Ask value, and tweaks.
`navigate` wraps screen commits in a choreography lock, captures shared-element positions, spawns a screen ghost, and later runs a FLIP animation via `FlipBridge`.
Theme effect derives an entire accent color family from a single hex tweak and writes CSS custom properties.
Feel effect maps density, warmth, zoning, radius, wall texture, reduced motion, and voice style to DOM attributes and CSS variables.
Transition effect maps tweak values to choreography CSS variables and a `no-flip` class.
Keyboard shortcuts include `g` for projects, `v` for Vi, `/` for Ask bar, and Esc to close.
Studio render uses a switch to map workspace names to workspace components grouped by zones (Project View, Mockup Studio, Build, Asset Lab, Operations, Account).
`completeWizard` opens the studio generation workspace and Vi panel but ignores the supplied `projectData`.
`askVi` from welcome seeds the wizard; `askSubmit` from studio dispatches a `vi-ask` event.
`JourneyBar` and `NextStep` are conditionally shown only in studio/build zones.
`TweaksPanel` is rendered only when `tweaks.showTweaks` is true, exposing live dials for motion, feel, assistant, generation, navigator, and theme.
standouts: Concurrent screen-transition choreography using `ScreenGhost` plus `FlipBridge` shared-element travel.
A single `tweaks` object drives color, spacing, motion, voice, density, and generation behavior across the app.
Unified Vi memory/identity overlay opened through multiple event types.
Global event bus (`vi-open-memory`, `vi-open-identity`, `vi-navigate`, `vi-ask`) coupling assistant surfaces to app state.
conventions: CSS custom properties and data attributes for runtime theming.
Function declarations for routing helpers (hoisted inside component).
concerns: Heavy reliance on undocumented window globals makes the file fragile and hard to type/test.
`navigate` uses a `setTimeout` lock without cleanup; rapid navigation could leave stale state or missed FLIP runs.
`completeWizard` receives `projectData` but does not use it.
`project` and `sequence` fall back to `data.projects[0]` without checking whether projects exist.
Large monolithic component mixes routing, theming, event handling, and render dispatch.
notes: This is the central mount point: it decides which screen the user sees, keeps the assistant context in sync, and turns a bag of tweak dials into live CSS and motion behavior.
questions: Where are `useTweaks`, `MS_DATA`, `MS_MOTION`, `MS_TOKENS`, `FlipBridge`, `ScreenGhost`, `ViContext`, `ViMemory`, and `StreamHost` defined or injected?
How are workspace components (e.g., `ProjectOverview`, `GenerationWorkspace`) made available—globally registered or imported elsewhere?
Why does `completeWizard` accept `projectData` if it is unused?
Is `sequence` intended to be consumed by `Shell` only, or should it influence workspace routing?
How does the Ask bar behave differently on welcome versus studio screens?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header comment says it is the screen router plus Vi assistant plus Ask bar, and the implementation does exactly that while also managing theme/motion tweaks.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a single, configurable product shell where navigation, the Vi assistant, and the entire visual personality (color, motion, density, voice) are controlled from one place and can be tuned live.
novelty: A runtime tweak system that simultaneously drives accent color derivation, FLIP-based screen transitions with a ghost overlay, and assistant voice/visibility—treating UI feel as a first-class configurable surface.
contribution: It contributes the top-level routing state, project context resolution, global assistant event plumbing, screen-transition choreography, and the tweak-to-CSS mapping that the rest of the application depends on for a consistent shell.
summary_for_embedding: Top-level React application shell for Counterpart that routes between welcome, projects overview, wizard, and studio workspaces, manages project and Vi assistant state, and applies a tweak-driven theme and motion system.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, tunable container where navigation, assistant presence, and visual feel are controlled consistently across the product.

symbols.does: Wraps a screen commit in exit choreography and shared-element travel.
symbols.description: It guards against double-fires with navLock, respects reduceMotion for instant transitions, captures shared-element positions via FlipBridge, spawns a ScreenGhost, commits the new screen, and releases the lock after the exit duration.

symbols.does: Opens the Vi memory overlay from a vi-open-memory event.
symbols.description: Reads event detail to set memory scope and aspect, defaulting scope to 'all' and aspect to 'learnings' when a scope is provided, then opens the memory panel.

symbols.does: Opens the Vi memory overlay from a vi-open-identity event.
symbols.description: Resets scope to 'all', uses the event's aspect if present, and opens the same memory overlay used by vi-open-memory, treating identity as a facet of memory.

symbols.does: Navigates the app from a vi-navigate event.
symbols.description: Closes the memory overlay, switches to the studio screen, and sets the workspace to the page specified in the event detail, letting Vi reach into real workspaces.

symbols.does: Parses a hex color string into an RGB array.
symbols.description: Normalizes shorthand hex to full form and returns [r, g, b] integers used by the theme effect to derive the accent family.

symbols.does: Linearly interpolates between two RGB arrays.
symbols.description: Returns a new RGB array where each channel is blended by factor t; used to create hover, press, ink, and gradient variants from the base accent.

symbols.does: Formats an RGB array as an rgb() CSS value.
symbols.description: Simple helper used to write CSS custom properties for accent colors in the theme effect.

symbols.does: Formats an RGB array and alpha as an rgba() CSS value.
symbols.description: Helper used to write translucent accent variants such as soft, faint, dashed, and glow tokens.
Focuses the Ask bar on '/', toggles Vi on 'v' while in studio, navigates to projects on 'g', and closes Vi/voice on Escape, ignoring input fields.
Handles the special 'settings' id by opening the settings workspace, otherwise sets the active project, switches to studio, and maps the project's phase to a default workspace.

symbols.does: Routes project opening through the navigation choreography.
symbols.description: Wraps commitOpenProject in navigate so the screen change plays the exit/enter animation.

symbols.does: Switches to the last visited page in a zone.
symbols.description: Looks up zoneLast for the zone id and falls back to data.firstPageOf, then sets the workspace.

symbols.does: Begins the wizard from a seed prompt.
symbols.description: Navigates to the wizard screen and stores the optional seed text in wizardSeed.

symbols.does: Finishes the wizard and lands in generation.
symbols.description: Navigates to the studio screen, opens the generation workspace, and immediately opens the Vi panel; note it does not use the supplied projectData.
Navigates back to the welcome screen via the choreography wrapper.

symbols.does: Switches to the projects overview screen and tab.
symbols.description: Navigates to the projects screen and sets the overview tab, defaulting to 'projects'.

symbols.does: Opens the projects overview at the projects tab.
Seeds the wizard from welcome-screen input.
symbols.description: Takes user text and starts the wizard with it, bridging the welcome Ask prompt into the wizard flow.
